articles.headline: Supreme Court Issues Comprehensive Directives for Forest Rights Act Implementation
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court, addressing a long-pending PIL, issued detailed guidelines to streamline the implementation of the Forest Rights Act (FRA), 2006.
• The directives aim to expedite the recognition and vesting of forest rights, particularly for Scheduled Tribes and Other Traditional Forest Dwellers (OTFDs).
• Key focus areas include improving the functioning of Forest Rights Committees (FRCs), ensuring transparency in claim processing, and preventing arbitrary evictions.
• The ruling emphasizes the need for convergence between FRA implementation and other development schemes to enhance tribal welfare and conservation efforts.
• States have been given a strict timeline to submit compliance reports on the measures taken.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes
Mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
prelimsPoints: Forest Rights Act (FRA) enacted in 2006.
FRA recognizes rights of forest dwelling Scheduled Tribes and Other Traditional Forest Dwellers (OTFDs).
Key rights under FRA include individual forest rights (IFR) and community forest rights (CFR).
Gram Sabha is the nodal body for initiating the process of determining the nature and extent of forest rights.
Ministry of Tribal Affairs is the nodal ministry for the implementation of FRA.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the challenges in effective implementation of FRA despite its progressive intent.
Discuss the role of judiciary in upholding the rights of tribal communities and addressing governance gaps.
Examine the potential impact of robust FRA implementation on tribal empowerment, forest conservation, and climate change mitigation.
Suggest administrative and legal reforms needed for better convergence between FRA and other development policies.
articles.imageDescription: A gavel resting on a law book, symbolizing a Supreme Court ruling, with a backdrop of a lush forest.
articles.trivia: The Forest Rights Act, 2006, is considered a landmark legislation as it addresses the historical injustice meted out to forest-dwelling communities.

articles.headline: India's Manufacturing PMI Touches Multi-Year High in February, Signals Robust Growth
articles.explainer:
• India's Manufacturing Purchasing Managers' Index (PMI) surged to a multi-year high in February, indicating strong expansion in the sector.
• The impressive growth was driven by a sharp increase in new orders, both domestic and export-oriented, and improved production levels.
• This positive trend reflects resilient domestic demand and a favorable global economic environment for Indian goods.
• The data suggests a healthy manufacturing sector contributing significantly to economic recovery and job creation.
• Input cost inflation, while present, remained manageable, allowing firms to maintain competitive pricing.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment.
Industrial policy
Effects of liberalization on the economy
prelimsPoints: PMI is an indicator of the economic health of the manufacturing and services sectors.
A PMI reading above 50 indicates expansion, while below 50 indicates contraction.
PMI is compiled by S&P Global in India.
It is based on five major indicators: new orders, inventory levels, production, supplier deliveries, and employment environment.
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors contributing to the recent surge in India's manufacturing PMI and its implications for economic growth.
Discuss the government's role through policies like 'Make in India' and PLI schemes in boosting the manufacturing sector.
Examine the challenges that the manufacturing sector still faces despite positive indicators, such as infrastructure bottlenecks and skill gaps.
Suggest measures to sustain this growth trajectory and integrate MSMEs into the global value chain.
articles.imageDescription: A factory floor with machinery in operation, symbolizing manufacturing growth.
articles.trivia: The first PMI was developed by the National Association of Purchasing Management (now ISM) in 1931.

articles.headline: India-France Strategic Dialogue Concludes, Emphasizing Defence Collaboration and Clean Energy Transition
articles.explainer:
• The annual India-France Strategic Dialogue concluded in New Delhi, reaffirming the strong bilateral partnership.
• Discussions primarily focused on enhancing cooperation in defence and security, including co-development and co-production of military hardware.
• Both nations reiterated their commitment to accelerating the clean energy transition, exploring collaborations in nuclear energy, hydrogen, and renewables.
• Regional and global security challenges, including the Indo-Pacific strategy and counter-terrorism, were also key agenda items.
• The dialogue underscored the shared vision for a stable, secure, and prosperous world order.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: India and France established a Strategic Partnership in 1998.
France is a key defence partner for India (e.g., Rafale jets, Scorpene submarines).
Both countries are active in the International Solar Alliance (ISA).
France is a permanent member of the UN Security Council.
The Indo-Pacific region is a shared strategic focus for both nations.
mainsPoints: Evaluate the significance of the India-France strategic partnership in shaping India's foreign policy and security architecture.
Discuss the potential for further collaboration in defence manufacturing and technology transfer between the two countries.
Analyze how India and France can jointly address global challenges such as climate change and maritime security in the Indo-Pacific.
Examine the role of bilateral dialogues in strengthening diplomatic ties and achieving shared objectives.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and France displayed side-by-side during a diplomatic meeting.
articles.trivia: France was one of the first countries to establish a strategic partnership with India after India's nuclear tests in 1998.

articles.headline: Five New Indian Wetlands Designated as Ramsar Sites, Boosting Conservation Efforts
articles.explainer:
• India has added five new sites to the list of Wetlands of International Importance, bringing the total number of Ramsar sites in the country to a new high.
• These newly designated sites include diverse ecosystems, ranging from freshwater lakes to coastal marshes, crucial for biodiversity and ecological services.
• The move underscores India's commitment to wetland conservation and sustainable management under the Ramsar Convention.
• Enhanced protection for these sites will help in preserving unique flora and fauna, mitigating climate change impacts, and supporting local livelihoods.
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change highlighted the importance of community participation in managing these ecologically sensitive areas.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Biodiversity and Climate Change.
prelimsPoints: The Ramsar Convention on Wetlands was signed in Ramsar, Iran, in 1971.
It is an intergovernmental treaty that provides the framework for national action and international cooperation for the conservation and wise use of wetlands and their resources.
India became a signatory to the Ramsar Convention in 1982.
Wetlands are crucial for water purification, flood control, and as habitats for migratory birds.
Chilika Lake in Odisha was the first Ramsar site designated in India.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological and economic significance of wetlands and the threats they face in India.
Evaluate the effectiveness of the Ramsar Convention in promoting wetland conservation globally and in India.
Analyze the challenges in designating and managing Ramsar sites in India, including land-use changes and pollution.
Suggest policy measures and community-based approaches for sustainable wetland management.
articles.imageDescription: A serene landscape of a wetland with migratory birds and lush vegetation.
articles.trivia: The Sundarbans, a part of the world's largest mangrove forest, is India's largest Ramsar site.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Advanced Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) Technology
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced the successful test flight of its latest Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) demonstrator.
• This critical test focused on validating autonomous landing capabilities and advanced aerodynamic characteristics for future space missions.
• RLV technology aims to significantly reduce the cost of space launches by enabling multiple uses of rocket components.
• The achievement marks a major step towards India's ambition for cost-effective and sustainable access to space.
• Further tests are planned to mature the technology for operational use in the coming decade.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of Space.
prelimsPoints: ISRO's RLV-TD (Technology Demonstrator) project aims to develop essential technologies for a fully reusable launch vehicle.
The primary goal of RLV technology is to reduce the cost of space access.
RLVs can perform multiple missions, similar to how airplanes are used repeatedly.
Key technologies include hypersonic flight, autonomous landing, and winged body re-entry.
SpaceX's Falcon 9 is a prominent example of an operational reusable rocket.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) technology for India's space program and its global standing.
Analyze the economic and environmental benefits of developing reusable launch systems compared to traditional expendable rockets.
Examine the challenges ISRO faces in developing and operationalizing RLV technology.
Suggest how RLV capabilities can further enhance India's commercial space sector and future human spaceflight missions.
articles.imageDescription: An artistic rendering of a reusable launch vehicle landing vertically.
articles.trivia: ISRO successfully conducted the first RLV-TD mission, HEX (Hypersonic Flight Experiment), in 2016.

articles.headline: Centre Unveils New Draft Rules for Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) released the draft rules for the recently enacted Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act), 2023.
• The rules provide detailed frameworks for consent mechanisms, data breach notifications, and the functioning of the Data Protection Board of India.
• Public consultation has been invited on these draft rules, aiming for a robust and inclusive regulatory framework.
• Key provisions address the rights of data principals and the obligations of data fiduciaries, focusing on transparency and accountability.
• The move is a crucial step towards operationalizing India's comprehensive data protection regime.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Role of IT in governance.
keyTerms: Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act), 2023
prelimsPoints: The Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, was passed by Parliament in August 2023.

It aims to provide for the processing of digital personal data in a manner that recognizes both the right of individuals to protect their personal data and the need to process personal data for lawful purposes.
The Act establishes the Data Protection Board of India.
The Puttaswamy judgment (2017) declared privacy a fundamental right under Article 21.
The Act defines 'personal data' and 'data fiduciary'.
mainsPoints: Analyze the key provisions of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, and its newly released draft rules.
Discuss the potential impact of this legislation on individual privacy, businesses, and government functioning in India.
Examine the challenges in implementing a comprehensive data protection framework in a digitally evolving economy.
Compare India's data protection regime with international standards like GDPR and assess its strengths and weaknesses.
articles.imageDescription: A stylized representation of digital data flowing, protected by a shield icon.
articles.trivia: The concept of 'data principal' in the DPDP Act refers to the individual to whom the personal data relates.

articles.headline: National Education Policy 2020 Implementation Review Highlights Transformative Progress
articles.explainer:
• A comprehensive review of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 implementation showcased significant progress across various levels of education.
• Key achievements include the integration of foundational literacy and numeracy, curriculum reforms, and increased focus on vocational education.
• The report highlighted improvements in teacher training programs and the adoption of technology-driven learning solutions.
• Challenges remain in ensuring equitable access and quality education in remote areas, necessitating further targeted interventions.
• The Ministry of Education reaffirmed its commitment to the NEP's vision of holistic, multidisciplinary, and future-ready education.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors.
prelimsPoints: National Education Policy 2020 replaced the 1986 National Policy on Education.
It proposes a new 5+3+3+4 curricular structure.
Emphasis on multidisciplinary education and flexibility in subject choices.
Aims to achieve 100% Gross Enrolment Ratio (GER) in pre-school to secondary level by 2030.
Establishment of National Research Foundation (NRF) is a key recommendation.
mainsPoints: Evaluate the transformative potential of NEP 2020 in addressing the contemporary challenges of the Indian education system.
Discuss the progress made in implementing key recommendations of NEP 2020 and identify the persistent bottlenecks.
Analyze the role of technology and teacher empowerment in achieving the goals set by the NEP.
Suggest strategies to ensure equitable access and quality education for all, especially in marginalized communities.
articles.imageDescription: Students engaged in a modern classroom setting with digital learning tools.
articles.trivia: The NEP 2020 is the third education policy in India's history, after those of 1968 and 1986.

articles.headline: Indian Coast Guard Inducts Advanced Patrol Vessels to Bolster Coastal Security
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Coast Guard (ICG) commissioned a new fleet of advanced offshore patrol vessels (OPVs) and interceptor boats.
• This induction significantly enhances India's maritime surveillance capabilities and response readiness along its extensive coastline.
• The new vessels are equipped with state-of-the-art sensors, communication systems, and weaponry for effective deterrence and interception.
• The move is part of a broader strategy to strengthen coastal security against threats like illegal fishing, smuggling, and maritime terrorism.
• Training and capacity building for ICG personnel are also being intensified to ensure optimal utilization of the new assets.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate.
prelimsPoints: The Indian Coast Guard (ICG) was established in 1978 as an armed force, search and rescue, and maritime law enforcement agency.
It operates under the Ministry of Defence.
The ICG's mandate includes protecting India's maritime interests in its Exclusive Economic Zone (EEZ).
Coastal security architecture was strengthened post-26/11 Mumbai attacks.
Key challenges include illegal immigration, smuggling, and unregulated fishing.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multi-faceted threats to India's coastal security and the institutional mechanisms in place to address them.
Evaluate the role of the Indian Coast Guard in safeguarding India's maritime interests and contributing to regional security.
Analyze the effectiveness of inter-agency coordination in coastal security management and suggest areas for improvement.
Examine how technological advancements and indigenous defence production can further bolster India's coastal defence capabilities.
articles.imageDescription: An Indian Coast Guard patrol vessel cruising at high speed on the ocean.
articles.trivia: The Indian Coast Guard is the fourth largest coast guard in the world.

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate, Citing Persistent Inflationary Pressures and Global Uncertainties
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) unanimously decided to keep the repo rate unchanged in its latest review.
• The decision was primarily driven by persistent concerns over elevated core inflation and emerging global economic uncertainties.
• RBI reiterated its commitment to withdrawing accommodation gradually to align inflation with the target while supporting growth.
• The central bank highlighted risks from volatile crude oil prices and geopolitical tensions impacting the inflation outlook.
• Analysts expect a cautious approach from RBI, with future rate actions dependent on evolving macroeconomic indicators.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment.
Monetary policy.
prelimsPoints: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is a six-member body responsible for fixing the benchmark interest rate (repo rate) in India.
RBI's primary objective is to maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
The current inflation target for RBI is 4% with a band of +/- 2%.
Repo rate is the rate at which the RBI lends money to commercial banks.
Reverse repo rate is the rate at which RBI borrows money from commercial banks.
mainsPoints: Analyze the rationale behind RBI's decision to maintain the repo rate and its implications for India's economic growth and inflation trajectory.
Discuss the challenges faced by RBI in balancing inflation control with the need to support economic recovery.
Examine the impact of global economic factors and geopolitical events on India's monetary policy decisions.
Suggest alternative tools and strategies RBI could employ to effectively manage liquidity and ensure financial stability.
articles.imageDescription: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) logo with currency notes in the background.
articles.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) was constituted in 2016 under the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934.

articles.headline: India Co-hosts BIMSTEC Summit, Calls for Enhanced Regional Economic and Security Cooperation
articles.explainer:
• India co-hosted the Bay of Bengal Initiative for Multi-Sectoral Technical and Economic Cooperation (BIMSTEC) Summit, emphasizing regional integration.
• Leaders discussed strengthening cooperation in trade, connectivity, energy, and counter-terrorism across the Bay of Bengal region.
• Prime Minister highlighted the importance of a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific and BIMSTEC's role in achieving this vision.
• Agreements were signed to boost maritime transport, facilitate customs cooperation, and enhance cultural exchanges among member states.
• The summit concluded with a joint declaration reaffirming commitment to a peaceful and prosperous Bay of Bengal community.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
India and its neighborhood- relations.
keyTerms: BIMSTEC (Bay of Bengal Initiative for Multi-Sectoral Technical and Economic Cooperation)
prelimsPoints: BIMSTEC comprises seven member states: Bangladesh, Bhutan, India, Myanmar, Nepal, Sri Lanka, and Thailand.
It was established in 1997 with the Bangkok Declaration.
BIMSTEC focuses on 14 priority sectors for cooperation, later streamlined to 7 sectors.
It serves as a bridge between South Asia and Southeast Asia.
India's 'Act East Policy' gives importance to BIMSTEC.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic significance of BIMSTEC for India's 'Act East Policy' and its role in regional connectivity and security.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities for BIMSTEC in achieving greater economic integration and addressing common security threats.
Evaluate the potential of BIMSTEC to emerge as a viable alternative to SAARC in promoting regional cooperation.
Suggest measures to enhance the effectiveness of BIMSTEC mechanisms and accelerate project implementation.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of BIMSTEC member countries displayed at a summit venue.
articles.trivia: Four of the BIMSTEC member states are also members of SAARC (India, Bangladesh, Bhutan, Nepal, Sri Lanka).

articles.headline: Government Launches New National Initiative for Enhanced Urban Air Quality Management
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change launched a new, comprehensive national initiative targeting improved urban air quality.
• The program integrates advanced monitoring technologies, stringent emission standards, and public awareness campaigns.
• Focus areas include industrial emissions control, vehicular pollution reduction, and management of construction dust and biomass burning.
• Collaboration with state governments and local bodies is central to the decentralized implementation strategy.
• The initiative aims to achieve significant reductions in particulate matter and other pollutants across major Indian cities.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors.
prelimsPoints: The National Clean Air Programme (NCAP) was launched in 2019 to reduce air pollution.
Air Quality Index (AQI) reports daily air quality based on eight pollutants.
Key pollutants include PM2.5, PM10, Ozone, Carbon Monoxide, Sulfur Dioxide, Nitrogen Dioxide, Ammonia, and Lead.
Vehicular emissions, industrial pollution, and biomass burning are major sources of air pollution in India.
The 'Graded Response Action Plan' (GRAP) is implemented in Delhi-NCR to tackle air pollution.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multi-dimensional challenges posed by urban air pollution in India and its impact on public health and economy.
Evaluate the effectiveness of existing government policies and programs in combating air pollution and identify their limitations.
Analyze the role of technology, citizen participation, and inter-state cooperation in achieving cleaner air in Indian cities.
Suggest innovative policy interventions and sustainable urban planning strategies for long-term air quality improvement.
articles.imageDescription: A city skyline shrouded in smog, with a clear sky on one side, symbolizing efforts to improve air quality.
articles.trivia: India is home to many of the world's most polluted cities, making air quality a critical public health issue.

articles.headline: Discussions on Uniform Civil Code Gain Momentum in Parliamentary Panel
articles.explainer:
• A parliamentary standing committee held extensive consultations on the Uniform Civil Code (UCC), indicating renewed momentum for the contentious issue.
• Stakeholders including legal experts, religious leaders, and civil society organizations presented diverse viewpoints on the feasibility and implications of UCC.
• The discussions aim to build consensus and explore potential pathways for a unified legal framework for personal laws.
• The UCC, enshrined in Article 44 of the Constitution, proposes a common set of laws governing marriage, divorce, inheritance, and adoption for all citizens.
• The government reiterated its commitment to Article 44, emphasizing the need for equitable and gender-just laws.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
prelimsPoints: Uniform Civil Code (UCC) is mentioned in Article 44 of the Indian Constitution.
Article 44 falls under the Directive Principles of State Policy (DPSP), making it non-justiciable but fundamental to governance.
UCC aims to replace diverse personal laws based on religion, custom, and tradition with a common set of laws.
Goa is currently the only Indian state with a Uniform Civil Code.
The Law Commission of India has previously examined the UCC.
mainsPoints: Discuss the constitutional mandate and historical context of the Uniform Civil Code (UCC) in India.
Analyze the arguments for and against the implementation of UCC, considering aspects of religious freedom, gender justice, and national integration.
Examine the challenges involved in drafting and implementing a UCC that respects India's diversity while ensuring equality.
Suggest a balanced approach to achieving the objectives of Article 44 without alienating diverse communities.
articles.imageDescription: A stylized representation of the Indian Constitution with diverse symbols representing different communities, converging into one.
articles.trivia: Dr. B.R. Ambedkar, while drafting the Constitution, advocated for a UCC but recognized the need for caution and consensus.

articles.headline: Indian Scientists Achieve Breakthrough in Indigenous Vaccine Development for Neglected Tropical Diseases
articles.explainer:
• A team of Indian scientists announced a significant breakthrough in developing an indigenous vaccine candidate for a neglected tropical disease (NTD).
• The novel vaccine, developed using advanced recombinant DNA technology, showed promising results in preclinical trials.
• This achievement holds immense potential for public health, particularly in regions where NTDs disproportionately affect vulnerable populations.
• The research emphasizes India's growing capabilities in biotechnology and pharmaceutical innovation.
• Funding and regulatory support are now being sought for accelerated clinical trials and eventual mass production.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of Biotechnology.
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health.
prelimsPoints: Neglected Tropical Diseases (NTDs) are a diverse group of communicable diseases prevalent in tropical and subtropical regions.
Examples include Dengue, Chikungunya, Kala-azar, Lymphatic Filariasis, and Leprosy.
India has a high burden of several NTDs.
Vaccine development involves several stages: exploratory, preclinical, clinical trials (Phase I, II, III), regulatory review, and manufacturing.
Recombinant DNA technology involves combining DNA from different organisms.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges posed by Neglected Tropical Diseases (NTDs) in India and their socio-economic impact on vulnerable populations.
Evaluate India's advancements in indigenous vaccine development and its potential to address global health disparities.
Analyze the role of public-private partnerships and international collaboration in accelerating research and development for NTDs.
Suggest policy measures to ensure affordable access to new vaccines and integrate NTD control into national health programs.
articles.imageDescription: A scientist in a lab coat working with a microscope and test tubes, symbolizing medical research.
articles.trivia: The World Health Organization (WHO) has a dedicated roadmap for the eradication and control of NTDs.

articles.headline: Government Intensifies Focus on Nutritional Security for Women and Children, Launches New Program
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Women and Child Development launched a new flagship program aimed at enhancing nutritional security among women and children.
• The initiative integrates health, sanitation, and food security interventions, adopting a multi-sectoral approach to tackle malnutrition.
• Special emphasis is placed on the first 1000 days of a child's life and the nutritional needs of pregnant and lactating mothers.
• The program leverages technology for real-time monitoring and aims to ensure last-mile delivery of essential services.
• Community health workers and Anganwadi centres are envisioned as crucial pillars for the program's success.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes.
prelimsPoints: POSHAN Abhiyaan (National Nutrition Mission) was launched in 2018 to improve nutritional outcomes.
Integrated Child Development Services (ICDS) scheme provides food, preschool education, primary healthcare, immunization, health check-up and referral services.
Anemia Mukt Bharat strategy aims to reduce anemia prevalence.
Malnutrition in children is measured by stunting (low height-for-age), wasting (low weight-for-height), and underweight (low weight-for-age).
Anganwadi Centres are key for delivering health and nutrition services at the grassroots level.
mainsPoints: Discuss the pervasive challenge of malnutrition among women and children in India and its intergenerational implications.
Evaluate the effectiveness of existing government schemes and policies in addressing nutritional deficiencies.
Analyze the multi-sectoral approach required to achieve comprehensive nutritional security, including health, sanitation, and food systems.
Suggest innovative strategies and community engagement models for improving maternal and child nutrition outcomes.
articles.imageDescription: A healthy child smiling, with a woman embracing her, symbolizing good nutrition and care.
articles.trivia: India's National Family Health Survey (NFHS) provides crucial data on health and nutrition indicators.

articles.headline: India's Agricultural Exports Witness Significant Growth Driven by New Market Access Agreements
articles.explainer:
• India's agricultural exports registered substantial growth in the last quarter, buoyed by new market access agreements with several countries.
• The surge was particularly noted in processed food, fresh fruits, and high-value agricultural products.
• Government initiatives, including export promotion schemes and infrastructure development for cold chains, played a crucial role.
• This growth contributes to increasing farmers' income and strengthening India's position as a global agricultural powerhouse.
• Efforts are underway to diversify the export basket and explore untapped markets in Africa and Latin America.
gsSubTopics: Major crops - cropping patterns in various parts of the country, - different types of irrigation and irrigation systems storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers.

Issues related to direct and indirect farm subsidies and minimum support prices; Public Distribution System— objectives, functioning, limitations, revamping; issues of buffer stocks and food security; Technology missions; economics of animal-rearing.
keyTerms: Agricultural and Processed Food Products Export Development Authority (APEDA)
prelimsPoints: APEDA (Agricultural and Processed Food Products Export Development Authority) is responsible for the promotion of export of agricultural and processed food products.
India is a major producer of several agricultural commodities like milk, spices, pulses, and rice.
The government aims to double farmers' income.
Cold chain infrastructure is vital for reducing post-harvest losses and enhancing agricultural exports.
Geographical Indication (GI) tags help in promoting specific agricultural products globally.
mainsPoints: Analyze the role of agricultural exports in boosting farmers' income and strengthening India's rural economy.
Discuss the government policies and infrastructure initiatives that facilitate the growth of agricultural exports.
Examine the challenges faced by Indian agricultural exporters in terms of quality standards, logistics, and global competition.
Suggest strategies for diversifying India's agricultural export basket and enhancing its competitiveness in international markets.
articles.imageDescription: Baskets overflowing with fresh produce, symbolizing agricultural abundance and exports.
articles.trivia: India is the world's largest producer of spices and pulses.

articles.headline: States Launch New Initiatives to Improve Ease of Doing Business and Attract Investment
articles.explainer:
• Several Indian states have announced new policy initiatives and reforms aimed at significantly improving their 'Ease of Doing Business' rankings.
• These reforms include simplification of regulatory procedures, online single-window clearances, and faster land acquisition processes.
• The initiatives are designed to attract domestic and foreign direct investment, fostering industrial growth and job creation.
• States are competing to create a more investment-friendly climate through incentives and efficient governance.
• The Centre lauded these efforts as crucial for achieving the national goal of becoming a top global investment destination.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Structure, organization and functioning of the Executive and the Judiciary Ministries and Departments of the Government; pressure groups and formal/informal associations and their role in the Polity.
prelimsPoints: The 'Ease of Doing Business' report was previously published by the World Bank (now discontinued).
DPIIT (Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade) conducts the 'Business Reform Action Plan' (BRAP) ranking for states.
Key parameters include starting a business, dealing with construction permits, registering property, paying taxes, and enforcing contracts.
States are focusing on digitalization of processes to improve efficiency.
Make in India initiative aims to boost domestic manufacturing.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of 'Ease of Doing Business' reforms at the state level for India's overall economic growth and global competitiveness.
Analyze the key areas where states are implementing reforms and the challenges they face in ensuring effective implementation.
Evaluate the role of inter-state competition and cooperative federalism in driving these reforms.
Suggest further policy measures and best practices that states can adopt to create a more attractive investment climate.
articles.imageDescription: A handshake over a blueprint, symbolizing collaboration and investment in business.
articles.trivia: Andhra Pradesh, Uttar Pradesh, and Telangana have often ranked high in India's state-level ease of doing business rankings.

articles.headline: India Pushes for Equitable Global Climate Finance Mechanisms in International Forums
articles.explainer:
• India actively participated in recent international climate forums, advocating for equitable and accessible global climate finance mechanisms.
• The Indian delegation emphasized the need for developed nations to fulfill their financial commitments to support developing countries' climate action.
• Discussions focused on innovative financing instruments, technology transfer, and capacity building for climate resilience.
• India highlighted its own ambitious climate goals and the substantial domestic investment required for its energy transition.
• The country reiterated its stance on 'Common but Differentiated Responsibilities and Respective Capabilities' (CBDR-RC) principle.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
keyTerms: Common but Differentiated Responsibilities and Respective Capabilities (CBDR-RC)
prelimsPoints: The UNFCCC (United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change) is the primary international treaty on climate change.
The Paris Agreement (2015) aims to limit global warming to well below 2, preferably to 1.5 degrees Celsius.
Developed countries pledged to mobilize $100 billion per year by 2020 for climate action in developing countries (not fully met).
The Green Climate Fund (GCF) is a key financial mechanism under the UNFCCC.
CBDR-RC is a principle recognizing different capabilities and historical responsibilities of countries in addressing climate change.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's position on global climate finance and its advocacy for equitable burden-sharing in addressing climate change.
Analyze the challenges in mobilizing and disbursing adequate climate finance to developing countries.
Evaluate the concept of 'Common but Differentiated Responsibilities and Respective Capabilities' (CBDR-RC) in the context of global climate negotiations.
Suggest mechanisms to ensure transparency, accountability, and accessibility of climate finance for effective climate action.
articles.imageDescription: A globe with hands protecting it, symbolizing international cooperation on climate change.
articles.trivia: India's updated Nationally Determined Contributions (NDCs) aim to reduce the emissions intensity of its GDP by 45% by 2030 from 2005 level.

articles.headline: India's Tourism Sector Records Unprecedented Growth, Driving Economic Revival
articles.explainer:
• India's tourism sector experienced unprecedented growth in the past year, surpassing pre-pandemic levels in both domestic and international arrivals.
• The robust recovery is attributed to government initiatives like 'Dekho Apna Desh', improved connectivity, and targeted promotional campaigns.
• The sector's revival has significantly contributed to job creation, foreign exchange earnings, and the growth of allied industries.
• Emphasis on sustainable tourism, cultural heritage, and adventure tourism is attracting a diverse range of visitors.
• Further investments in infrastructure and skill development are planned to sustain this growth momentum and enhance visitor experience.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: The Ministry of Tourism is the nodal agency for the development and promotion of tourism in India.
'Dekho Apna Desh' initiative promotes domestic tourism.
The 'Swadesh Darshan Scheme' aims at integrated development of theme-based tourist circuits.
India offers diverse tourism products: cultural, adventure, medical, spiritual, MICE (Meetings, Incentives, Conferences, Exhibitions).
Tourism is a major contributor to India's GDP and employment.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multi-faceted contributions of the tourism sector to India's economy and socio-cultural development.
Analyze the key factors driving the recent growth in India's tourism sector and the challenges it still faces.
Evaluate the role of government policies and infrastructure development in promoting sustainable and inclusive tourism.
Suggest innovative strategies to further enhance India's global appeal as a tourist destination and ensure equitable benefits for local communities.
articles.imageDescription: A diverse collage of famous Indian tourist destinations, including historical monuments and natural landscapes.
articles.trivia: The Taj Mahal is India's most visited tourist attraction.

articles.headline: Indian Researchers Develop Advanced AI Model for Accurate Disaster Prediction and Management
articles.explainer:
• A team of Indian researchers has developed an advanced Artificial Intelligence (AI) model for more accurate prediction of natural disasters.
• The AI system integrates satellite imagery, meteorological data, and historical patterns to forecast events like floods, droughts, and heatwaves.
• This indigenous technology aims to enhance India's disaster preparedness and minimize loss of life and property.
• The model offers real-time insights and decision-support tools for disaster management authorities.
• Further collaboration with national and state disaster response agencies is underway for pilot implementation.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Disaster and disaster management.
prelimsPoints: India is highly vulnerable to various natural disasters like floods, droughts, cyclones, and earthquakes.
The National Disaster Management Authority (NDMA) is the apex body for disaster management in India.
The Sendai Framework for Disaster Risk Reduction (2015-2030) guides global efforts.
Early warning systems are crucial for effective disaster preparedness.
AI and ML are increasingly used in predictive analytics across various sectors.
mainsPoints: Discuss the critical role of technology, particularly Artificial Intelligence, in enhancing India's disaster prediction and management capabilities.
Analyze the challenges in integrating advanced AI models into existing disaster response frameworks at national and local levels.
Evaluate the potential of indigenous technological solutions in building climate resilience and achieving disaster risk reduction targets.
Suggest policy measures to promote research, development, and deployment of AI-based tools for effective disaster governance.
articles.imageDescription: A stylized map of India showing weather patterns and digital data overlays, symbolizing AI-driven disaster prediction.
articles.trivia: India's IMD (India Meteorological Department) provides weather forecasts and warnings for various natural hazards.

highlights: Supreme Court issues comprehensive directives for effective implementation of the Forest Rights Act, 2006, ensuring tribal rights.
India's Manufacturing PMI surges to a multi-year high in February, signaling robust economic growth and strong demand.
India-France Strategic Dialogue strengthens defence collaboration and clean energy transition, reaffirming strong bilateral ties.
Five new Indian wetlands designated as Ramsar Sites, boosting national and international efforts for wetland conservation.
ISRO successfully tests advanced Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) technology, marking a major step towards cost-effective space access.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the Forest Rights Act (FRA), 2006, consider the following statements:
1. The Act recognizes both Individual Forest Rights (IFR) and Community Forest Rights (CFR).
2. The Gram Sabha is the first authority to initiate the process for determining the nature and extent of individual or community forest rights.
3. The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change is the nodal ministry for the implementation of the Act.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
options: (a) 1 only
(b) 1 and 2 only
(c) 2 and 3 only
(d) 1, 2 and 3
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: The FRA, 2006, recognizes both IFR and CFR. Statement 2 is correct: The Gram Sabha is indeed the statutory body responsible for initiating the process of determining forest rights. Statement 3 is incorrect: The Ministry of Tribal Affairs is the nodal ministry for the implementation of the Forest Rights Act, 2006.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following statements best describes the Purchasing Managers' Index (PMI)?
options: (a) It measures the inflation rate in the manufacturing sector.
(b) It is an indicator of the economic health of the manufacturing and services sectors.
(c) It tracks the government's expenditure on infrastructure projects.
(d) It assesses the foreign direct investment inflows into a country.
prelimsMocks.answer: (b) It is an indicator of the economic health of the manufacturing and services sectors.
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Purchasing Managers' Index (PMI) is an economic indicator derived from monthly surveys of private sector companies. A PMI above 50 indicates expansion, while a reading below 50 suggests contraction. It reflects the overall health and business conditions of the manufacturing and services sectors.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following about the Bay of Bengal Initiative for Multi-Sectoral Technical and Economic Cooperation (BIMSTEC):
1. It comprises five South Asian and two Southeast Asian countries.
2. India's 'Look East Policy' gives importance to BIMSTEC.
3. All member countries are also members of the SAARC grouping.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
options: (a) 1 only
(b) 2 only
(c) 1 and 2 only
(d) 1, 2 and 3
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: BIMSTEC has seven members: Bangladesh, Bhutan, India, Nepal, Sri Lanka (South Asia) and Myanmar, Thailand (Southeast Asia). Statement 2 is correct: India's 'Act East Policy' (formerly Look East Policy) places significant emphasis on BIMSTEC for regional connectivity and cooperation. Statement 3 is incorrect: While five BIMSTEC members (India, Bangladesh, Bhutan, Nepal, Sri Lanka) are also in SAARC, Myanmar and Thailand are not SAARC members.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following is NOT a primary objective of developing Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) technology by ISRO?
options: (a) Reducing the cost of space launches.
(b) Enabling multiple uses of rocket components.
(c) Enhancing the payload capacity of rockets significantly.
(d) Achieving sustainable access to space.
prelimsMocks.answer: (c) Enhancing the payload capacity of rockets significantly.
prelimsMocks.explanation: The primary objectives of RLV technology are to reduce launch costs, enable multiple uses of components, and achieve sustainable and routine access to space. While technological advancements might indirectly lead to some payload efficiencies, significantly enhancing payload capacity is not the direct or primary objective of reusability itself. Often, reusability comes with a trade-off in payload capacity compared to expendable versions of the same rocket.

prelimsMocks.question: Regarding the Uniform Civil Code (UCC) in India, which of the following statements is correct?
options: (a) It is a fundamental right guaranteed under Article 21 of the Constitution.
(b) It is explicitly mentioned as a Directive Principle of State Policy under Article 44.
(c) Goa is the only Indian state where a Uniform Civil Code is not implemented.
(d) It aims to unify criminal laws across all religions.
prelimsMocks.answer: (b) It is explicitly mentioned as a Directive Principle of State Policy under Article 44.
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement (a) is incorrect; UCC is not a fundamental right. Statement (b) is correct; Article 44 states that 'The State shall endeavour to secure for the citizens a Uniform Civil Code throughout the territory of India.' Statement (c) is incorrect; Goa is the only Indian state that currently has a Uniform Civil Code. Statement (d) is incorrect; UCC aims to unify personal laws (marriage, divorce, inheritance, adoption), not criminal laws, which are already largely uniform across India.

mainsMocks.question: The Supreme Court's recent directives on the Forest Rights Act, 2006, highlight the persistent gaps in its implementation. Critically analyze the challenges in realizing the objectives of FRA and suggest measures for effective tribal empowerment and forest conservation.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population; GS3: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce FRA and its objectives. Discuss implementation challenges (e.g., bureaucratic hurdles, lack of awareness, conflicting land claims, environmental concerns). Analyze the role of judiciary. Suggest measures like strengthening Gram Sabhas, inter-departmental coordination, capacity building, and convergence with other schemes. Conclude with the importance of balanced development.

mainsMocks.question: India's ambition for cost-effective and sustainable access to space hinges on the success of its Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) program. Discuss the strategic and economic significance of RLV technology for India and the challenges ISRO faces in its development and operationalization.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology; Awareness in the fields of Space.
mainsMocks.approach: Begin with the context of RLV test. Explain its strategic importance (e.g., reduced launch costs, increased launch frequency, commercial competitiveness, national security). Detail economic benefits (e.g., commercial space market, resource optimization). Outline challenges (e.g., technological complexity, funding, material science, re-entry dynamics, regulatory framework). Conclude on India's potential to become a leader in this domain.

mainsMocks.question: The ongoing discussions on the Uniform Civil Code (UCC) reflect a complex interplay of constitutional mandate, religious freedoms, and gender justice. Examine the constitutional basis for UCC and critically discuss the arguments for and against its implementation in India's diverse socio-cultural landscape.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure; Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce UCC and Article 44 as a DPSP. Explain its constitutional origins and the intent of the framers. Present arguments 'for' UCC (gender justice, national integration, secularism, equality before law, simplifying legal system). Present arguments 'against' UCC (threat to religious freedom, diversity, potential for majoritarianism, practical difficulties, lack of consensus). Conclude with a balanced perspective, emphasizing the need for broad consultation and a gradual, inclusive approach.

quickBytes.text: The Forest Rights Act, 2006, recognizes rights over forest land for Scheduled Tribes and Other Traditional Forest Dwellers.
A PMI (Purchasing Managers' Index) reading above 50 indicates economic expansion in the manufacturing or services sector.
India and France established their Strategic Partnership in 1998, focusing on defence, space, and nuclear energy.
The Ramsar Convention, signed in 1971 in Iran, is an intergovernmental treaty for wetland conservation.
ISRO's RLV (Reusable Launch Vehicle) program aims to reduce space launch costs and enable sustainable access to space.
The Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, establishes the Data Protection Board of India.
NEP 2020 proposes a new 5+3+3+4 curricular structure for school education in India.
The Indian Coast Guard (ICG) operates under the Ministry of Defence, safeguarding India's maritime interests.
The RBI's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is a six-member body that determines the benchmark repo rate.
BIMSTEC connects five South Asian and two Southeast Asian countries for multi-sectoral cooperation.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Issues Comprehensive Directives for Forest Rights Act Implementation
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court, addressing a long-pending PIL, issued detailed guidelines to streamline the implementation of the Forest Rights Act (FRA), 2006.
• The directives aim to expedite the recognition and vesting of forest rights, particularly for Scheduled Tribes and Other Traditional Forest Dwellers (OTFDs).
• Key focus areas include improving the functioning of Forest Rights Committees (FRCs), ensuring transparency in claim processing, and preventing arbitrary evictions.
• The ruling emphasizes the need for convergence between FRA implementation and other development schemes to enhance tribal welfare and conservation efforts.
• States have been given a strict timeline to submit compliance reports on the measures taken.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes
Mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
prelimsPoints: Forest Rights Act (FRA) enacted in 2006.
FRA recognizes rights of forest dwelling Scheduled Tribes and Other Traditional Forest Dwellers (OTFDs).
Key rights under FRA include individual forest rights (IFR) and community forest rights (CFR).
Gram Sabha is the nodal body for initiating the process of determining the nature and extent of forest rights.
Ministry of Tribal Affairs is the nodal ministry for the implementation of FRA.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the challenges in effective implementation of FRA despite its progressive intent.
Discuss the role of judiciary in upholding the rights of tribal communities and addressing governance gaps.
Examine the potential impact of robust FRA implementation on tribal empowerment, forest conservation, and climate change mitigation.
Suggest administrative and legal reforms needed for better convergence between FRA and other development policies.
GS2.imageDescription: A gavel resting on a law book, symbolizing a Supreme Court ruling, with a backdrop of a lush forest.
GS2.trivia: The Forest Rights Act, 2006, is considered a landmark legislation as it addresses the historical injustice meted out to forest-dwelling communities.

GS2.headline: India-France Strategic Dialogue Concludes, Emphasizing Defence Collaboration and Clean Energy Transition
GS2.explainer:
• The annual India-France Strategic Dialogue concluded in New Delhi, reaffirming the strong bilateral partnership.
• Discussions primarily focused on enhancing cooperation in defence and security, including co-development and co-production of military hardware.
• Both nations reiterated their commitment to accelerating the clean energy transition, exploring collaborations in nuclear energy, hydrogen, and renewables.
• Regional and global security challenges, including the Indo-Pacific strategy and counter-terrorism, were also key agenda items.
• The dialogue underscored the shared vision for a stable, secure, and prosperous world order.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: India and France established a Strategic Partnership in 1998.
France is a key defence partner for India (e.g., Rafale jets, Scorpene submarines).
Both countries are active in the International Solar Alliance (ISA).
France is a permanent member of the UN Security Council.
The Indo-Pacific region is a shared strategic focus for both nations.
mainsPoints: Evaluate the significance of the India-France strategic partnership in shaping India's foreign policy and security architecture.
Discuss the potential for further collaboration in defence manufacturing and technology transfer between the two countries.
Analyze how India and France can jointly address global challenges such as climate change and maritime security in the Indo-Pacific.
Examine the role of bilateral dialogues in strengthening diplomatic ties and achieving shared objectives.
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of India and France displayed side-by-side during a diplomatic meeting.
GS2.trivia: France was one of the first countries to establish a strategic partnership with India after India's nuclear tests in 1998.

GS2.headline: Centre Unveils New Draft Rules for Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) released the draft rules for the recently enacted Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act), 2023.
• The rules provide detailed frameworks for consent mechanisms, data breach notifications, and the functioning of the Data Protection Board of India.
• Public consultation has been invited on these draft rules, aiming for a robust and inclusive regulatory framework.
• Key provisions address the rights of data principals and the obligations of data fiduciaries, focusing on transparency and accountability.
• The move is a crucial step towards operationalizing India's comprehensive data protection regime.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Role of IT in governance.
keyTerms: Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act), 2023
prelimsPoints: The Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, was passed by Parliament in August 2023.

It aims to provide for the processing of digital personal data in a manner that recognizes both the right of individuals to protect their personal data and the need to process personal data for lawful purposes.
The Act establishes the Data Protection Board of India.
The Puttaswamy judgment (2017) declared privacy a fundamental right under Article 21.
The Act defines 'personal data' and 'data fiduciary'.
mainsPoints: Analyze the key provisions of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, and its newly released draft rules.
Discuss the potential impact of this legislation on individual privacy, businesses, and government functioning in India.
Examine the challenges in implementing a comprehensive data protection framework in a digitally evolving economy.
Compare India's data protection regime with international standards like GDPR and assess its strengths and weaknesses.
GS2.imageDescription: A stylized representation of digital data flowing, protected by a shield icon.
GS2.trivia: The concept of 'data principal' in the DPDP Act refers to the individual to whom the personal data relates.

GS2.headline: National Education Policy 2020 Implementation Review Highlights Transformative Progress
GS2.explainer:
• A comprehensive review of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 implementation showcased significant progress across various levels of education.
• Key achievements include the integration of foundational literacy and numeracy, curriculum reforms, and increased focus on vocational education.
• The report highlighted improvements in teacher training programs and the adoption of technology-driven learning solutions.
• Challenges remain in ensuring equitable access and quality education in remote areas, necessitating further targeted interventions.
• The Ministry of Education reaffirmed its commitment to the NEP's vision of holistic, multidisciplinary, and future-ready education.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors.
prelimsPoints: National Education Policy 2020 replaced the 1986 National Policy on Education.
It proposes a new 5+3+3+4 curricular structure.
Emphasis on multidisciplinary education and flexibility in subject choices.
Aims to achieve 100% Gross Enrolment Ratio (GER) in pre-school to secondary level by 2030.
Establishment of National Research Foundation (NRF) is a key recommendation.
mainsPoints: Evaluate the transformative potential of NEP 2020 in addressing the contemporary challenges of the Indian education system.
Discuss the progress made in implementing key recommendations of NEP 2020 and identify the persistent bottlenecks.
Analyze the role of technology and teacher empowerment in achieving the goals set by the NEP.
Suggest strategies to ensure equitable access and quality education for all, especially in marginalized communities.
GS2.imageDescription: Students engaged in a modern classroom setting with digital learning tools.
GS2.trivia: The NEP 2020 is the third education policy in India's history, after those of 1968 and 1986.

GS2.headline: India Co-hosts BIMSTEC Summit, Calls for Enhanced Regional Economic and Security Cooperation
GS2.explainer:
• India co-hosted the Bay of Bengal Initiative for Multi-Sectoral Technical and Economic Cooperation (BIMSTEC) Summit, emphasizing regional integration.
• Leaders discussed strengthening cooperation in trade, connectivity, energy, and counter-terrorism across the Bay of Bengal region.
• Prime Minister highlighted the importance of a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific and BIMSTEC's role in achieving this vision.
• Agreements were signed to boost maritime transport, facilitate customs cooperation, and enhance cultural exchanges among member states.
• The summit concluded with a joint declaration reaffirming commitment to a peaceful and prosperous Bay of Bengal community.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
India and its neighborhood- relations.
keyTerms: BIMSTEC (Bay of Bengal Initiative for Multi-Sectoral Technical and Economic Cooperation)
prelimsPoints: BIMSTEC comprises seven member states: Bangladesh, Bhutan, India, Myanmar, Nepal, Sri Lanka, and Thailand.
It was established in 1997 with the Bangkok Declaration.
BIMSTEC focuses on 14 priority sectors for cooperation, later streamlined to 7 sectors.
It serves as a bridge between South Asia and Southeast Asia.
India's 'Act East Policy' gives importance to BIMSTEC.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic significance of BIMSTEC for India's 'Act East Policy' and its role in regional connectivity and security.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities for BIMSTEC in achieving greater economic integration and addressing common security threats.
Evaluate the potential of BIMSTEC to emerge as a viable alternative to SAARC in promoting regional cooperation.
Suggest measures to enhance the effectiveness of BIMSTEC mechanisms and accelerate project implementation.
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of BIMSTEC member countries displayed at a summit venue.
GS2.trivia: Four of the BIMSTEC member states are also members of SAARC (India, Bangladesh, Bhutan, Nepal, Sri Lanka).

GS2.headline: Discussions on Uniform Civil Code Gain Momentum in Parliamentary Panel
GS2.explainer:
• A parliamentary standing committee held extensive consultations on the Uniform Civil Code (UCC), indicating renewed momentum for the contentious issue.
• Stakeholders including legal experts, religious leaders, and civil society organizations presented diverse viewpoints on the feasibility and implications of UCC.
• The discussions aim to build consensus and explore potential pathways for a unified legal framework for personal laws.
• The UCC, enshrined in Article 44 of the Constitution, proposes a common set of laws governing marriage, divorce, inheritance, and adoption for all citizens.
• The government reiterated its commitment to Article 44, emphasizing the need for equitable and gender-just laws.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
prelimsPoints: Uniform Civil Code (UCC) is mentioned in Article 44 of the Indian Constitution.
Article 44 falls under the Directive Principles of State Policy (DPSP), making it non-justiciable but fundamental to governance.
UCC aims to replace diverse personal laws based on religion, custom, and tradition with a common set of laws.
Goa is currently the only Indian state with a Uniform Civil Code.
The Law Commission of India has previously examined the UCC.
mainsPoints: Discuss the constitutional mandate and historical context of the Uniform Civil Code (UCC) in India.
Analyze the arguments for and against the implementation of UCC, considering aspects of religious freedom, gender justice, and national integration.
Examine the challenges involved in drafting and implementing a UCC that respects India's diversity while ensuring equality.
Suggest a balanced approach to achieving the objectives of Article 44 without alienating diverse communities.
GS2.imageDescription: A stylized representation of the Indian Constitution with diverse symbols representing different communities, converging into one.
GS2.trivia: Dr. B.R. Ambedkar, while drafting the Constitution, advocated for a UCC but recognized the need for caution and consensus.

GS2.headline: Government Intensifies Focus on Nutritional Security for Women and Children, Launches New Program
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Women and Child Development launched a new flagship program aimed at enhancing nutritional security among women and children.
• The initiative integrates health, sanitation, and food security interventions, adopting a multi-sectoral approach to tackle malnutrition.
• Special emphasis is placed on the first 1000 days of a child's life and the nutritional needs of pregnant and lactating mothers.
• The program leverages technology for real-time monitoring and aims to ensure last-mile delivery of essential services.
• Community health workers and Anganwadi centres are envisioned as crucial pillars for the program's success.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes.
prelimsPoints: POSHAN Abhiyaan (National Nutrition Mission) was launched in 2018 to improve nutritional outcomes.
Integrated Child Development Services (ICDS) scheme provides food, preschool education, primary healthcare, immunization, health check-up and referral services.
Anemia Mukt Bharat strategy aims to reduce anemia prevalence.
Malnutrition in children is measured by stunting (low height-for-age), wasting (low weight-for-height), and underweight (low weight-for-age).
Anganwadi Centres are key for delivering health and nutrition services at the grassroots level.
mainsPoints: Discuss the pervasive challenge of malnutrition among women and children in India and its intergenerational implications.
Evaluate the effectiveness of existing government schemes and policies in addressing nutritional deficiencies.
Analyze the multi-sectoral approach required to achieve comprehensive nutritional security, including health, sanitation, and food systems.
Suggest innovative strategies and community engagement models for improving maternal and child nutrition outcomes.
GS2.imageDescription: A healthy child smiling, with a woman embracing her, symbolizing good nutrition and care.
GS2.trivia: India's National Family Health Survey (NFHS) provides crucial data on health and nutrition indicators.

GS2.headline: States Launch New Initiatives to Improve Ease of Doing Business and Attract Investment
GS2.explainer:
• Several Indian states have announced new policy initiatives and reforms aimed at significantly improving their 'Ease of Doing Business' rankings.
• These reforms include simplification of regulatory procedures, online single-window clearances, and faster land acquisition processes.
• The initiatives are designed to attract domestic and foreign direct investment, fostering industrial growth and job creation.
• States are competing to create a more investment-friendly climate through incentives and efficient governance.
• The Centre lauded these efforts as crucial for achieving the national goal of becoming a top global investment destination.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Structure, organization and functioning of the Executive and the Judiciary Ministries and Departments of the Government; pressure groups and formal/informal associations and their role in the Polity.
prelimsPoints: The 'Ease of Doing Business' report was previously published by the World Bank (now discontinued).
DPIIT (Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade) conducts the 'Business Reform Action Plan' (BRAP) ranking for states.
Key parameters include starting a business, dealing with construction permits, registering property, paying taxes, and enforcing contracts.
States are focusing on digitalization of processes to improve efficiency.
Make in India initiative aims to boost domestic manufacturing.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of 'Ease of Doing Business' reforms at the state level for India's overall economic growth and global competitiveness.
Analyze the key areas where states are implementing reforms and the challenges they face in ensuring effective implementation.
Evaluate the role of inter-state competition and cooperative federalism in driving these reforms.
Suggest further policy measures and best practices that states can adopt to create a more attractive investment climate.
GS2.imageDescription: A handshake over a blueprint, symbolizing collaboration and investment in business.
GS2.trivia: Andhra Pradesh, Uttar Pradesh, and Telangana have often ranked high in India's state-level ease of doing business rankings.

GS2.headline: India Pushes for Equitable Global Climate Finance Mechanisms in International Forums
GS2.explainer:
• India actively participated in recent international climate forums, advocating for equitable and accessible global climate finance mechanisms.
• The Indian delegation emphasized the need for developed nations to fulfill their financial commitments to support developing countries' climate action.
• Discussions focused on innovative financing instruments, technology transfer, and capacity building for climate resilience.
• India highlighted its own ambitious climate goals and the substantial domestic investment required for its energy transition.
• The country reiterated its stance on 'Common but Differentiated Responsibilities and Respective Capabilities' (CBDR-RC) principle.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
keyTerms: Common but Differentiated Responsibilities and Respective Capabilities (CBDR-RC)
prelimsPoints: The UNFCCC (United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change) is the primary international treaty on climate change.
The Paris Agreement (2015) aims to limit global warming to well below 2, preferably to 1.5 degrees Celsius.
Developed countries pledged to mobilize $100 billion per year by 2020 for climate action in developing countries (not fully met).
The Green Climate Fund (GCF) is a key financial mechanism under the UNFCCC.
CBDR-RC is a principle recognizing different capabilities and historical responsibilities of countries in addressing climate change.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's position on global climate finance and its advocacy for equitable burden-sharing in addressing climate change.
Analyze the challenges in mobilizing and disbursing adequate climate finance to developing countries.
Evaluate the concept of 'Common but Differentiated Responsibilities and Respective Capabilities' (CBDR-RC) in the context of global climate negotiations.
Suggest mechanisms to ensure transparency, accountability, and accessibility of climate finance for effective climate action.
GS2.imageDescription: A globe with hands protecting it, symbolizing international cooperation on climate change.
GS2.trivia: India's updated Nationally Determined Contributions (NDCs) aim to reduce the emissions intensity of its GDP by 45% by 2030 from 2005 level.

GS3.headline: India's Manufacturing PMI Touches Multi-Year High in February, Signals Robust Growth
GS3.explainer:
• India's Manufacturing Purchasing Managers' Index (PMI) surged to a multi-year high in February, indicating strong expansion in the sector.
• The impressive growth was driven by a sharp increase in new orders, both domestic and export-oriented, and improved production levels.
• This positive trend reflects resilient domestic demand and a favorable global economic environment for Indian goods.
• The data suggests a healthy manufacturing sector contributing significantly to economic recovery and job creation.
• Input cost inflation, while present, remained manageable, allowing firms to maintain competitive pricing.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment.
Industrial policy
Effects of liberalization on the economy
prelimsPoints: PMI is an indicator of the economic health of the manufacturing and services sectors.
A PMI reading above 50 indicates expansion, while below 50 indicates contraction.
PMI is compiled by S&P Global in India.
It is based on five major indicators: new orders, inventory levels, production, supplier deliveries, and employment environment.
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors contributing to the recent surge in India's manufacturing PMI and its implications for economic growth.
Discuss the government's role through policies like 'Make in India' and PLI schemes in boosting the manufacturing sector.
Examine the challenges that the manufacturing sector still faces despite positive indicators, such as infrastructure bottlenecks and skill gaps.
Suggest measures to sustain this growth trajectory and integrate MSMEs into the global value chain.
GS3.imageDescription: A factory floor with machinery in operation, symbolizing manufacturing growth.
GS3.trivia: The first PMI was developed by the National Association of Purchasing Management (now ISM) in 1931.

GS3.headline: Five New Indian Wetlands Designated as Ramsar Sites, Boosting Conservation Efforts
GS3.explainer:
• India has added five new sites to the list of Wetlands of International Importance, bringing the total number of Ramsar sites in the country to a new high.
• These newly designated sites include diverse ecosystems, ranging from freshwater lakes to coastal marshes, crucial for biodiversity and ecological services.
• The move underscores India's commitment to wetland conservation and sustainable management under the Ramsar Convention.
• Enhanced protection for these sites will help in preserving unique flora and fauna, mitigating climate change impacts, and supporting local livelihoods.
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change highlighted the importance of community participation in managing these ecologically sensitive areas.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Biodiversity and Climate Change.
prelimsPoints: The Ramsar Convention on Wetlands was signed in Ramsar, Iran, in 1971.
It is an intergovernmental treaty that provides the framework for national action and international cooperation for the conservation and wise use of wetlands and their resources.
India became a signatory to the Ramsar Convention in 1982.
Wetlands are crucial for water purification, flood control, and as habitats for migratory birds.
Chilika Lake in Odisha was the first Ramsar site designated in India.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological and economic significance of wetlands and the threats they face in India.
Evaluate the effectiveness of the Ramsar Convention in promoting wetland conservation globally and in India.
Analyze the challenges in designating and managing Ramsar sites in India, including land-use changes and pollution.
Suggest policy measures and community-based approaches for sustainable wetland management.
GS3.imageDescription: A serene landscape of a wetland with migratory birds and lush vegetation.
GS3.trivia: The Sundarbans, a part of the world's largest mangrove forest, is India's largest Ramsar site.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Advanced Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) Technology
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced the successful test flight of its latest Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) demonstrator.
• This critical test focused on validating autonomous landing capabilities and advanced aerodynamic characteristics for future space missions.
• RLV technology aims to significantly reduce the cost of space launches by enabling multiple uses of rocket components.
• The achievement marks a major step towards India's ambition for cost-effective and sustainable access to space.
• Further tests are planned to mature the technology for operational use in the coming decade.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of Space.
prelimsPoints: ISRO's RLV-TD (Technology Demonstrator) project aims to develop essential technologies for a fully reusable launch vehicle.
The primary goal of RLV technology is to reduce the cost of space access.
RLVs can perform multiple missions, similar to how airplanes are used repeatedly.
Key technologies include hypersonic flight, autonomous landing, and winged body re-entry.
SpaceX's Falcon 9 is a prominent example of an operational reusable rocket.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) technology for India's space program and its global standing.
Analyze the economic and environmental benefits of developing reusable launch systems compared to traditional expendable rockets.
Examine the challenges ISRO faces in developing and operationalizing RLV technology.
Suggest how RLV capabilities can further enhance India's commercial space sector and future human spaceflight missions.
GS3.imageDescription: An artistic rendering of a reusable launch vehicle landing vertically.
GS3.trivia: ISRO successfully conducted the first RLV-TD mission, HEX (Hypersonic Flight Experiment), in 2016.

GS3.headline: Indian Coast Guard Inducts Advanced Patrol Vessels to Bolster Coastal Security
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Coast Guard (ICG) commissioned a new fleet of advanced offshore patrol vessels (OPVs) and interceptor boats.
• This induction significantly enhances India's maritime surveillance capabilities and response readiness along its extensive coastline.
• The new vessels are equipped with state-of-the-art sensors, communication systems, and weaponry for effective deterrence and interception.
• The move is part of a broader strategy to strengthen coastal security against threats like illegal fishing, smuggling, and maritime terrorism.
• Training and capacity building for ICG personnel are also being intensified to ensure optimal utilization of the new assets.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate.
prelimsPoints: The Indian Coast Guard (ICG) was established in 1978 as an armed force, search and rescue, and maritime law enforcement agency.
It operates under the Ministry of Defence.
The ICG's mandate includes protecting India's maritime interests in its Exclusive Economic Zone (EEZ).
Coastal security architecture was strengthened post-26/11 Mumbai attacks.
