Key challenges include illegal immigration, smuggling, and unregulated fishing.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multi-faceted threats to India's coastal security and the institutional mechanisms in place to address them.
Evaluate the role of the Indian Coast Guard in safeguarding India's maritime interests and contributing to regional security.
Analyze the effectiveness of inter-agency coordination in coastal security management and suggest areas for improvement.
Examine how technological advancements and indigenous defence production can further bolster India's coastal defence capabilities.
GS3.imageDescription: An Indian Coast Guard patrol vessel cruising at high speed on the ocean.
GS3.trivia: The Indian Coast Guard is the fourth largest coast guard in the world.

GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate, Citing Persistent Inflationary Pressures and Global Uncertainties
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) unanimously decided to keep the repo rate unchanged in its latest review.
• The decision was primarily driven by persistent concerns over elevated core inflation and emerging global economic uncertainties.
• RBI reiterated its commitment to withdrawing accommodation gradually to align inflation with the target while supporting growth.
• The central bank highlighted risks from volatile crude oil prices and geopolitical tensions impacting the inflation outlook.
• Analysts expect a cautious approach from RBI, with future rate actions dependent on evolving macroeconomic indicators.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment.
Monetary policy.
prelimsPoints: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is a six-member body responsible for fixing the benchmark interest rate (repo rate) in India.
RBI's primary objective is to maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
The current inflation target for RBI is 4% with a band of +/- 2%.
Repo rate is the rate at which the RBI lends money to commercial banks.
Reverse repo rate is the rate at which RBI borrows money from commercial banks.
mainsPoints: Analyze the rationale behind RBI's decision to maintain the repo rate and its implications for India's economic growth and inflation trajectory.
Discuss the challenges faced by RBI in balancing inflation control with the need to support economic recovery.
Examine the impact of global economic factors and geopolitical events on India's monetary policy decisions.
Suggest alternative tools and strategies RBI could employ to effectively manage liquidity and ensure financial stability.
GS3.imageDescription: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) logo with currency notes in the background.
GS3.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) was constituted in 2016 under the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934.

GS3.headline: Government Launches New National Initiative for Enhanced Urban Air Quality Management
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change launched a new, comprehensive national initiative targeting improved urban air quality.
• The program integrates advanced monitoring technologies, stringent emission standards, and public awareness campaigns.
• Focus areas include industrial emissions control, vehicular pollution reduction, and management of construction dust and biomass burning.
• Collaboration with state governments and local bodies is central to the decentralized implementation strategy.
• The initiative aims to achieve significant reductions in particulate matter and other pollutants across major Indian cities.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors.
prelimsPoints: The National Clean Air Programme (NCAP) was launched in 2019 to reduce air pollution.
Air Quality Index (AQI) reports daily air quality based on eight pollutants.
Key pollutants include PM2.5, PM10, Ozone, Carbon Monoxide, Sulfur Dioxide, Nitrogen Dioxide, Ammonia, and Lead.
Vehicular emissions, industrial pollution, and biomass burning are major sources of air pollution in India.
The 'Graded Response Action Plan' (GRAP) is implemented in Delhi-NCR to tackle air pollution.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multi-dimensional challenges posed by urban air pollution in India and its impact on public health and economy.
Evaluate the effectiveness of existing government policies and programs in combating air pollution and identify their limitations.
Analyze the role of technology, citizen participation, and inter-state cooperation in achieving cleaner air in Indian cities.
Suggest innovative policy interventions and sustainable urban planning strategies for long-term air quality improvement.
GS3.imageDescription: A city skyline shrouded in smog, with a clear sky on one side, symbolizing efforts to improve air quality.
GS3.trivia: India is home to many of the world's most polluted cities, making air quality a critical public health issue.

GS3.headline: Indian Scientists Achieve Breakthrough in Indigenous Vaccine Development for Neglected Tropical Diseases
GS3.explainer:
• A team of Indian scientists announced a significant breakthrough in developing an indigenous vaccine candidate for a neglected tropical disease (NTD).
• The novel vaccine, developed using advanced recombinant DNA technology, showed promising results in preclinical trials.
• This achievement holds immense potential for public health, particularly in regions where NTDs disproportionately affect vulnerable populations.
• The research emphasizes India's growing capabilities in biotechnology and pharmaceutical innovation.
• Funding and regulatory support are now being sought for accelerated clinical trials and eventual mass production.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of Biotechnology.
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health.
prelimsPoints: Neglected Tropical Diseases (NTDs) are a diverse group of communicable diseases prevalent in tropical and subtropical regions.
Examples include Dengue, Chikungunya, Kala-azar, Lymphatic Filariasis, and Leprosy.
India has a high burden of several NTDs.
Vaccine development involves several stages: exploratory, preclinical, clinical trials (Phase I, II, III), regulatory review, and manufacturing.
Recombinant DNA technology involves combining DNA from different organisms.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges posed by Neglected Tropical Diseases (NTDs) in India and their socio-economic impact on vulnerable populations.
Evaluate India's advancements in indigenous vaccine development and its potential to address global health disparities.
Analyze the role of public-private partnerships and international collaboration in accelerating research and development for NTDs.
Suggest policy measures to ensure affordable access to new vaccines and integrate NTD control into national health programs.
GS3.imageDescription: A scientist in a lab coat working with a microscope and test tubes, symbolizing medical research.
GS3.trivia: The World Health Organization (WHO) has a dedicated roadmap for the eradication and control of NTDs.

GS3.headline: India's Agricultural Exports Witness Significant Growth Driven by New Market Access Agreements
GS3.explainer:
• India's agricultural exports registered substantial growth in the last quarter, buoyed by new market access agreements with several countries.
• The surge was particularly noted in processed food, fresh fruits, and high-value agricultural products.
• Government initiatives, including export promotion schemes and infrastructure development for cold chains, played a crucial role.
• This growth contributes to increasing farmers' income and strengthening India's position as a global agricultural powerhouse.
• Efforts are underway to diversify the export basket and explore untapped markets in Africa and Latin America.
gsSubTopics: Major crops - cropping patterns in various parts of the country, - different types of irrigation and irrigation systems storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers.

Issues related to direct and indirect farm subsidies and minimum support prices; Public Distribution System— objectives, functioning, limitations, revamping; issues of buffer stocks and food security; Technology missions; economics of animal-rearing.
keyTerms: Agricultural and Processed Food Products Export Development Authority (APEDA)
prelimsPoints: APEDA (Agricultural and Processed Food Products Export Development Authority) is responsible for the promotion of export of agricultural and processed food products.
India is a major producer of several agricultural commodities like milk, spices, pulses, and rice.
The government aims to double farmers' income.
Cold chain infrastructure is vital for reducing post-harvest losses and enhancing agricultural exports.
Geographical Indication (GI) tags help in promoting specific agricultural products globally.
mainsPoints: Analyze the role of agricultural exports in boosting farmers' income and strengthening India's rural economy.
Discuss the government policies and infrastructure initiatives that facilitate the growth of agricultural exports.
Examine the challenges faced by Indian agricultural exporters in terms of quality standards, logistics, and global competition.
Suggest strategies for diversifying India's agricultural export basket and enhancing its competitiveness in international markets.
GS3.imageDescription: Baskets overflowing with fresh produce, symbolizing agricultural abundance and exports.
GS3.trivia: India is the world's largest producer of spices and pulses.

GS3.headline: India's Tourism Sector Records Unprecedented Growth, Driving Economic Revival
GS3.explainer:
• India's tourism sector experienced unprecedented growth in the past year, surpassing pre-pandemic levels in both domestic and international arrivals.
• The robust recovery is attributed to government initiatives like 'Dekho Apna Desh', improved connectivity, and targeted promotional campaigns.
• The sector's revival has significantly contributed to job creation, foreign exchange earnings, and the growth of allied industries.
• Emphasis on sustainable tourism, cultural heritage, and adventure tourism is attracting a diverse range of visitors.
• Further investments in infrastructure and skill development are planned to sustain this growth momentum and enhance visitor experience.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: The Ministry of Tourism is the nodal agency for the development and promotion of tourism in India.
'Dekho Apna Desh' initiative promotes domestic tourism.
The 'Swadesh Darshan Scheme' aims at integrated development of theme-based tourist circuits.
India offers diverse tourism products: cultural, adventure, medical, spiritual, MICE (Meetings, Incentives, Conferences, Exhibitions).
Tourism is a major contributor to India's GDP and employment.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multi-faceted contributions of the tourism sector to India's economy and socio-cultural development.
Analyze the key factors driving the recent growth in India's tourism sector and the challenges it still faces.
Evaluate the role of government policies and infrastructure development in promoting sustainable and inclusive tourism.
Suggest innovative strategies to further enhance India's global appeal as a tourist destination and ensure equitable benefits for local communities.
GS3.imageDescription: A diverse collage of famous Indian tourist destinations, including historical monuments and natural landscapes.
GS3.trivia: The Taj Mahal is India's most visited tourist attraction.

GS3.headline: Indian Researchers Develop Advanced AI Model for Accurate Disaster Prediction and Management
GS3.explainer:
• A team of Indian researchers has developed an advanced Artificial Intelligence (AI) model for more accurate prediction of natural disasters.
• The AI system integrates satellite imagery, meteorological data, and historical patterns to forecast events like floods, droughts, and heatwaves.
• This indigenous technology aims to enhance India's disaster preparedness and minimize loss of life and property.
• The model offers real-time insights and decision-support tools for disaster management authorities.
• Further collaboration with national and state disaster response agencies is underway for pilot implementation.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Disaster and disaster management.
prelimsPoints: India is highly vulnerable to various natural disasters like floods, droughts, cyclones, and earthquakes.
The National Disaster Management Authority (NDMA) is the apex body for disaster management in India.
The Sendai Framework for Disaster Risk Reduction (2015-2030) guides global efforts.
Early warning systems are crucial for effective disaster preparedness.
AI and ML are increasingly used in predictive analytics across various sectors.
mainsPoints: Discuss the critical role of technology, particularly Artificial Intelligence, in enhancing India's disaster prediction and management capabilities.
Analyze the challenges in integrating advanced AI models into existing disaster response frameworks at national and local levels.
Evaluate the potential of indigenous technological solutions in building climate resilience and achieving disaster risk reduction targets.
Suggest policy measures to promote research, development, and deployment of AI-based tools for effective disaster governance.
GS3.imageDescription: A stylized map of India showing weather patterns and digital data overlays, symbolizing AI-driven disaster prediction.
GS3.trivia: India's IMD (India Meteorological Department) provides weather forecasts and warnings for various natural hazards.
